mission.title: consul: CORS requests fail with a 405 method not allow for OPTIONS
mission.description: CORS requests fail with a 405 method not allow for OPTIONS. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current consul deployment
steps.description:
Verify your consul version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
helm list -n consul 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working consul installation.

steps.title: Review consul configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant consul configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl get configmap -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=consul
```
Hi,

I tried to setup CORS headers:

```
{
  "http_api_response_headers": {
    "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
    "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "POST, GET, OPTIONS, DELETE, PUT"
  }
}
```

I verified that those headers are return during a

steps.title: Apply the fix for CORS requests fail with a 405 method not allow for OPTIONS
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl get events -n consul --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "CORS requests fail with a 405 method not allow for OPTIONS" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Enables development of JavaScript API clients that use CORS requests. Also, responding to OPTIONS requests with 200 OK is just a good baseline behavior. 

This needs unit tests, but I wanted to get feedback on the way it's implemented first. I'm also happy to rebase & squash when the time comes.
codeSnippets: curl 'http://192.168.111.30:8500/v1/kv/lb/extra_hosts/opi' -X OPTIONS -H 'Host: 192.168.111.30:8500' -H 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10.10; rv:39.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/39.0' -H 'Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8' -H 'Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.5' --compressed -H 'DNT: 1' -H 'Origin: http://local.dev:8101' -H 'Access-Control-Request-Method: PUT' -H 'Connection: keep-alive'

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with consul installed or the issue environment reproducible.
